notes: Minimal valid manifest (supported_contract_versions [1] only): a single extension point omitting completion_mode/interaction_mode entirely; schema does not enforce the documented defaults (sync/none) via required, only via description, so omission must remain valid.
v1-only manifest explicitly declaring completion_mode: sync and interaction_mode: none, plus the optional top-level description/publisher/license fields and an extension-point confirm_text.
supported_contract_versions [1, 2] manifest with a deferred, non-launch extension point declaring the required endpoints.operation_status alongside endpoints.invoke.
supported_contract_versions [1, 2] manifest with a deferred extension point requiring browser continuation (interaction_mode: launch_url) and endpoints.operation_status present.
Manifest mixing a sync extension point with deferred non-launch and deferred launch_url extension points under one endpoints/supported_contract_versions declaration.

supported_contract_versions [1] only, extension point declares interaction_mode: launch_url with completion_mode omitted (defaults sync); the standalone manifest schema permits this combination — any cross-document semantic constraint on pairing interaction_mode with deferred completion is outside this schema's scope.

Prior invented deployed-v1-incompatible shape: top-level contract_version + actions[] instead of manifest_version/runtime/endpoints/extension_points. Must be rejected as this repository now models the deployed v1 manifest shape exactly.
An extension_points entry MUST NOT declare its own endpoints object; endpoints is exclusively a top-level manifest property shared by all extension points.
extension_points[].action_id is required.
completion_mode: deferred requires supported_contract_versions to contain 2, even when endpoints.operation_status is already present; supported_contract_versions here is [1] only.
completion_mode: deferred requires endpoints.operation_status, even when supported_contract_versions already contains 2; endpoints here declares only invoke.
Missing required top-level extension_points array.
Domain-specific top-level field (subject_area) not permitted by additionalProperties: false; this repository's schemas are domain-neutral (G1).
Well-formed deployed-shape v1 invoke request: user/project_id/tree/source present, no plugin_id and no opaque input wrapper.
contract_version must be const 1 for this schema; 2 is rejected here (v2 has its own request schema).

Prior invented shape carrying plugin_id and an opaque input wrapper, and omitting the required user/project_id/tree/source fields, must be rejected (additionalProperties: false plus missing required fields).
user, project_id, tree, and source are all required on the deployed v1 request shape.
Minimal deployed-shape response: status ok with no result object.
status ok with an optional result object carrying url and external_id.
operation_id is a v2-only concept; v1 response schema has additionalProperties: false.
Prior invented state/result_mode envelope must be rejected: the deployed v1 response schema only recognizes status/error_code/message/result, and has no state or result_mode property at all.
status must be one of ok|failed; 'running' (a v2 state-machine vocabulary value) is not a member of this enum.
Well-formed v2 invoke request (deployed v1 fields plus contract_version 2) with an HTTPS host_api_base_url origin (no path/query/fragment).
http://127.0.0.1 is the sole documented non-HTTPS exception for host_api_base_url, for local conformance testing only.
Plain HTTP, non-localhost host_api_base_url MUST be rejected (HTTPS-origin rule).
host_api_base_url MUST be an origin only (no path/query/fragment) — anti-SSRF constraint.
host_api_base_url is required for contract_version 2.
plugin_id MUST NOT appear on the invoke request (deployed v1 shape carried no plugin_id, and v2 only adds host_api_base_url on top of it); additionalProperties: false rejects it.
Non-launch variant, awaiting_user, launch_url correctly absent, nested under result.
Non-launch variant already running with result_mode production; launch_url absent.
Launch variant with launch_url present inside result.

The standalone accepted-response schema carries no interaction_mode property at all, so it cannot itself require launch_url for any given state; an awaiting_user result omitting launch_url is schema-valid. Whether a specific action's manifest (interaction_mode: launch_url) requires launch_url here is a cross-document rule enforced elsewhere, not by this schema.

Symmetric to the above: launch_url present on a running result is schema-valid even though the originating manifest action might declare interaction_mode: none; that pairing is a cross-document rule, not enforced by this standalone schema.
estimated_duration_seconds is not part of this closed schema (checked at the nested result level).
launch_url MUST be an absolute HTTPS URL.
interaction_mode MUST NOT appear nested inside result; the accepted envelope has no interaction_mode property anywhere (additionalProperties: false on result).
interaction_mode MUST NOT appear at the top level of the accepted envelope either (additionalProperties: false at the root).
Prior invented flat {operation_id, state, result_mode, interaction_mode} shape must be rejected: the deployed-compatible accepted envelope requires status: 'accepted' plus a nested result object.
First callback event for an operation; sequence 1, state running, minimal fields, result_mode mock.
Terminal succeeded event with result_mode production, result_url, provider, and full progress.
progress.percent at the lower boundary (0), inclusive.
progress.percent at the upper boundary (100), inclusive.
result_mode (mock) is independent of the optional result_url: a mock-mode event may still carry a result_url. Demonstrates result_mode and result_url are orthogonal, not coupled.
course_id is a domain-specific field; this schema is domain-neutral (G1) and additionalProperties: false.
'pending' is a host-only bookkeeping status, never a valid plugin-authored state.
event_id is required (idempotency key).
Callback events use sequencePositive (>=1); sequence 0 is reserved for the non-callback initial status snapshot.
Negative sequence numbers are never valid.
progress.percent must be within [0, 100]; 150 exceeds the maximum.
progress carries no domain-specific sub-fields (additionalProperties: false); eta_seconds is not permitted.
result_mode is an execution-mode enum limited to mock|production; 'url' is not a member (this rejection is now purely a closed-enum violation, no longer a 'missing result_url' pairing rule).
result_mode is required and limited to mock|production; 'none' is not a member of this enum.
result_url MUST be an absolute HTTPS URL.
error_message exceeds maxLength: 500.
event_id contains spaces/punctuation outside the ^[A-Za-z0-9_.:-]{1,64}$ pattern.
Attempting to smuggle a shared secret via an additional property; additionalProperties: false rejects it structurally (docs/security.md: secrets are never in any payload).
sequence 0 synchronous snapshot; state MUST be (and here is) awaiting_user, result_mode mock.
sequence 1 snapshot reflecting the first delivered callback event, result_mode production.
Mirrors the completion-event result_mode/result_url independence check at the operation-status shape: result_mode mock together with a present result_url, plus provider and full progress.
sequence 0 MUST report state awaiting_user; 'running' at sequence 0 is structurally disallowed.
Negative sequence numbers are never valid, even though this schema allows 0.
internal_queue_position is host-internal bookkeeping, never part of the plugin-reported status body.
event_id is required on the status snapshot too (identifies the latest reflected event, or a seq-0 placeholder).
Shared progress $def parity check: operation-status must reject the same progress-level extra field as completion-event.
result_mode is required and limited to mock|production; 'none' is not a member of this enum (parity with completion-event).
result_mode is limited to mock|production; 'url' is not a member of this enum (parity with completion-event).
Minimal accepted response; all optional detail flags omitted.
Idempotent replay of an already-recorded event_id.
Stale event: authenticated and well-formed, but not applied (terminal-freeze or out-of-order regression).
retry_after_seconds is not part of this closed schema.
accepted is the single required, load-bearing signal for this response.
Well-formed claim set with the fixed purpose literal and all 9 required claims.
purpose MUST equal the fixed literal plugin_interaction; any other value fails the const check (audience-confusion guard).
No additional claims are permitted by this schema (additionalProperties: false); 'role' is rejected.
jti is required (single-use replay guard).
Well-formed 3-segment JWS compact serialization string.
assertion must match the 3-segment JWS compact pattern; a bare opaque string does not.
csrf_token does not belong on the exchange *request* (it is only ever returned in the exchange response).
Well-formed exchange response echoing invocation_id and a csrf_token >=16 chars.
csrf_token has minLength: 16; this one is far shorter.
session_id is not part of this closed schema (the session lives in the Set-Cookie header, not the body).
The final HTTPS Location preserves the launch query and carries one compact JWS in the sole fixed fragment field.
The interaction assertion is never transported in the query and a handoff Location must contain the fixed fragment.
The handoff fragment contains exactly one contenttree_assertion field and no unrelated fragment fields.
Duplicate contenttree_assertion fragment fields are malformed and must be rejected.
Browser continuation handoff is HTTPS-only.
